ddls:
CREATE TABLE "redis_servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Redis server */,
  "ip" TEXT /* IP address of the Redis server */,
  "port" INTEGER /* Port on which the Redis server is running */,
  "hostname" TEXT /* Hostname of the Redis server */,
  "tag" TEXT /* Tag or label for the Redis server */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the Redis server */,
  "build_id" TEXT /* Build ID of the Redis server */,
  "mode" TEXT /* Mode of the Redis server (e.g., standalone, cluster) */,
  "os" TEXT /* Operating system of the Redis server */,
  "architecture" TEXT /* Architecture of the Redis server */,
  "multiplexing_api" TEXT /* Multiplexing API used by the Redis server */,
  "gcc_version" TEXT /* GCC version used to compile the Redis server */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sector the server belongs to */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the client the server belongs to */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the server details */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the server details */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the server was created */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the server */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the server (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the cluster if the server is part of a Redis cluster */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_redis_servers_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("sector_id") REFERENCES sectors ("sector_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_redis_servers_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the client */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the client */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region the client is located in */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the client was created */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the client */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the client details */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the client details */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the client (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clients_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector */,
  "sector_name" TEXT /* Name of the sector */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sector */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the sector was created */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the sector */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the sector details */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the sector details */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the sector (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Country code of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the region was created */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the region */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the region details */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the region details */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the region (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the Redis server */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the metric */,
  "uptime" INTEGER /* Uptime of the Redis server in seconds */,
  "connected_clients" INTEGER /* Number of connected clients */,
  "git_sha1" TEXT /* Git SHA1 of the Redis server */,
  "git_dirty_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the Redis server is dirty (0 for clean, 1 for dirty) */,
  "run_id" TEXT /* Run ID of the Redis server */,
  "process_id" INTEGER /* Process ID of the Redis server */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Current memory usage of the Redis server */,
  "cpu_load" REAL /* Current CPU load of the Redis server */,
  "io_ops_per_sec" INTEGER /* I/O operations per second */,
  "hit_ratio" REAL /* Cache hit ratio */,
  "latency" REAL /* Average latency of Redis commands */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES redis_servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_info" (
  "network_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network entry */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the Redis server */,
  "ip" TEXT /* IP address of the Redis server */,
  "port" INTEGER /* Port on which the Redis server is running */,
  "hostname" TEXT /* Hostname of the Redis server */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol used by the Redis server */,
  "asn" INTEGER /* Autonomous System Number (ASN) of the Redis server */,
  "geo" TEXT /* Geographical location of the Redis server */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the Redis server is located */,
  "naics" INTEGER /* NAICS (North American Industry Classification System) code of the Redis server */,
  "sic" INTEGER /* SIC (Standard Industrial Classification) code of the Redis server */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the network information */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the network information */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the network information (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_info_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES redis_servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, engineer, analyst) */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the client the user belongs to */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user was created */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the user */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user details */,
  "last_updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the user details */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the Redis server */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Redis server being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the Redis server was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client making the access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES redis_servers ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "server_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration entry */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the Redis server */,
  "config_data" TEXT /* Configuration data of the Redis server */,
  "config_version" TEXT /* Version of the configuration */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration was last updated */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who last updated the configuration */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the configuration (e.g., active, archived) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration changes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_server_configurations_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES redis_servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the Redis server */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., high load, downtime) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of the alert (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* User who resolved the alert */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the alert (e.g., server, client) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES redis_servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the Redis server */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of the report (e.g., performance, security) */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data of the report */,
  "generated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the report was generated */,
  "generated_by" TEXT /* User who generated the report */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the report (e.g., generated, reviewed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES redis_servers ("server_id")
)
